gate_claim: the full Phase C candidate is correct and finalize-gate-safe against AC-C: (dedup) no two mechanisms check the same bytes — edition-sync --check no longer duplicates validate-script-sync's COMMON_SCRIPTS/byte-group check, and the adaptive-node L1 no longer spawns a --resume-check subprocess on the hash-match fast-path; (integrity) per-mutation plan integrity is preserved with the tamper test red-to-green and a tampered plan still refusing plan_integrity_failed before mutation; (hooks) both advisory hooks (pre-commit, write-lane) are deleted across all four editions together with their six hooks.json PreToolUse entries, the install.sh lingering-copy cleanup, the two install-manifest SUPPORT_HOOKS entries, and every four-chain assertion that named them (validate-workflow-contracts + its codex byte twin, validate-kaola-workflow-contracts, the gitlab/gitea contract validators, validate-script-sync's two hook byte-copy groups, the four edition walkthroughs' hook test sections + the hook-id-set assertion, and test-install-model-rendering's expected id set), so all four edition chains stay green; the surviving compact-context + subagent-dispatch-log hooks and their assertions are untouched; (docs) the README/api/architecture/conventions/workflow-state-contract/CLAUDE.md delta removes only the two deleted hooks' mentions with no provenance leakage and CLAUDE.md stays under 200 lines, and CHANGELOG documents the guard dedup + hook deletion under [Unreleased]; (scope) opencode/kimi are correctly NOT re-synced here; and all four edition chains are green over the final tree
gate_surface: the full accumulated Phase C candidate vs run base 0a9f652a across all four editions — edition-sync dedup (n1), the adaptive-node L1 integrity fast-path + tamper test (n3), the two-hook deletion and its full wiring/assertion/manifest surface (n5), and the documentation delta (n6) — reviewed against AC-C and the four-chain-green evidence

## Finding digest provenance (all sha256, computed in the worktree)

- precondition_digest (all five) = sha256 of kaola-workflow/issue-725/.cache/n5-hook-deletion.md — the producer evidence establishing the precondition state (8 hook files deleted, PreToolUse emptied from all six hooks.json). expected_digest (all five) = sha256 of the verbatim gate_claim string above (verified byte-exact against the review context's acceptance_evidence claim_digest 0a3429c3..., no trailing newline).
- input_digest per finding = sha256 of the full bytes of the primary-anchor file at the candidate tree. Anchor object_ids are git sha1 blob ids of the worktree files (the candidate snapshot is a git add -A temp-index write-tree, so these are the candidate-tree ids); start/end are byte offsets of the defective region.
- observed_digest per finding = sha256 of the exact captured failure line: R1 "AssertionError [ERR_ASSERTION]: hooks.json must have entries for event PreToolUse" (thrown at test-gitlab-workflow-scripts.js:2065, exit 1); R2 "AssertionError [ERR_ASSERTION]: hooks.json must have entries for PreToolUse" (test-gitea-workflow-scripts.js:2030, exit 1); R3 "TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'push')" (test-validate-script-sync.js:142, exit 1); R4 "Error: ENOENT: no such file or directory, open '/Volumes/WorkspaceA/ylminiserver/workspace/kaola-workflow/.kw/worktrees/issue-725/hooks/kaola-workflow-pre-commit.sh'" (thrown in sync-opencode-edition.js writeHooks :741, surfacing as test-install-adaptive-config.js exit 1); R5 "Error: #409: expected the four managed hook commands, saw 2" (simulate-kaola-workflow-walkthrough.js:736, exit 1).

## Finding details

- R1 (gitlab chain red). plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/scripts/test-gitlab-workflow-scripts.js:2063-2069 hard-asserts a populated PreToolUse event with at least one kaola-workflow: entry in the installed hooks.json; the candidate correctly emptied PreToolUse in plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/config/hooks.json, so the assertion fails. Reproduced standalone: `node plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/scripts/test-gitlab-workflow-scripts.js` exits 1 at :2065. The file is shelled in-chain by the gitlab walkthrough (simulate-gitlab-workflow-walkthrough.js:1643), a step of `npm run test:kaola-workflow:gitlab`, so the gitlab chain cannot go green. The file is in NO plan node's declared write set — replan routing. Fix shape (per n5's own gap note): drop 'PreToolUse' from the inline event array.
- R2 (gitea chain red). plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/scripts/test-gitea-workflow-scripts.js:2027-2033, identical shape reading plugins/kaola-workflow-gitea/config/hooks.json. Reproduced standalone: exits 1 at :2030. Shelled in-chain at simulate-gitea-workflow-walkthrough.js:1723. Unowned file — replan routing.
- R3 (claude chain red, step 2). scripts/test-validate-script-sync.js:139-147 (section 6a, the hooks.json-family drift RED-PROOF) reads the REAL hooks/hooks.json and executes rootHooks.hooks.PreToolUse.push({...}) at :142 to plant a fixture drift entry. The candidate removed the PreToolUse key from hooks/hooks.json entirely, so the expression throws TypeError at module load and the whole test file exits 1, redding `npm run test:kaola-workflow:claude` at its second step. n5's residual grep sweep could not catch this: its needles were pre-commit|write-lane, and this file's load-bearing literal is PreToolUse. Unowned file — replan routing. Fix shape: plant the fixture entry on a surviving event (or create the PreToolUse array in the fixture only).
- R4 (claude chain red, step 11). scripts/sync-opencode-edition.js:73-77 HOOK_SCRIPTS still lists 'kaola-workflow-pre-commit.sh' and 'kaola-workflow-write-lane.sh'; writeHooks (:741) does readFileSync on each from canonical hooks/, now ENOENT. This is NOT additive-only exposure: the in-chain claude step scripts/test-install-adaptive-config.js (secondary anchor, runOpencodeInstall at :33-36) shells `bash install-opencode.sh --yes --no-scripts --target ...`, which regenerates the .opencode tree via this sync script, so the claude chain itself reds. This falsifies the plan's deferral assumption behind the (scope) element — the candidate correctly did NOT re-sync opencode (no scope violation), but the opencode SYNC SCRIPT is exercised inside the claude chain, so the two deleted basenames cannot stay in HOOK_SCRIPTS until Phase D. Unowned file — replan routing (planner decides: drop the two entries now, or decouple the in-chain test).
- R5 (codex chain red — n5-owned, reopen-in-place candidate). plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/simulate-kaola-workflow-walkthrough.js:736 (test409StableHomeSurvivesDirDeletion) still asserts commandCount >= 4 ("expected the four managed hook commands"); after the PreToolUse removal the installed hooks.json carries 2 managed commands, so `npm run test:kaola-workflow:codex` reds at its walkthrough step (reproduced; log shows testAC1HooksJson PASSED then the :736 throw). This file IS in n5's declared write set — n5 fixed testAC1HooksJson's EVENTS array but missed this second hook-count assertion in the same file (n5 ran only the canonical walkthrough, not the codex one, per its evidence). Graph-maximal owner n5 — reopens n5 in place per the gate direction. :736 is the only remaining stale hook-count assertion in the file (grep for count-bearing hook asserts returns only :736; :721/:729 are per-command shape asserts, count-agnostic).

Consolidation note: R1/R2/R3/R5 share the root cause class (hook-deletion assertion surfaces missed by the write-set/needle scoping) but are kept as separate findings per the dispatch's explicit one-finding-per-file anchoring instruction, since primary_anchor.path drives per-file repair ownership routing and R5 has a different owner than R1-R4.

## Review narrative

Process: read the review context (discovery phase, epoch 1, no prior findings, empty validation_obligations, claim_root_base 0a9f652a), the archived Phase A certifier evidence (finding shape template), all four upstream evidence files (nonces echoed above), and the issue #725 Phase C section + AC-C via `gh issue view 725`. Then independently verified the full candidate diff vs 0a9f652a (42 tracked paths: 8 deletions + 34 modifications; maps exactly onto the declared n1/n3/n5/n6 write sets with zero strays; kaola-workflow/issue-725/ untracked state dir expected) and re-executed the verification surface myself.

Per-claim-element verification:

1. (dedup) PASS. edition-sync.js diff removes checkMirrors entirely (function, runCheck call, export, the now-unused checkByteIdenticalGroup import) and narrows the success log to the 12 aggregator ports; runWrite untouched; validate-script-sync.js remains the sole COMMON_SCRIPTS/byte-group checker. adaptive-node L1 skips the --resume-check subprocess on an in-process hash match (test #725a asserts zero validator resume-check shells on the fast path). `node scripts/edition-sync.js --check` green (12 ports), `node scripts/test-edition-sync.js` green (40 assertions), `node scripts/validate-script-sync.js` green (22/26/5/2/7 — 26 byte groups, correctly down 2).
2. (integrity) PASS. The canonical L1 hunk recomputes via the plan-validator's exported computePlanHash (plan-validator.js:2568 — the same hasher revalidateForResume calls at :560, same full-content input, so the covered-byte region cannot drift), extracts the marker via the local planHashFromContent (:2992), and on mismatch/missing-marker/any-error falls back to the full --resume-check shell refusing plan_integrity_failed exactly as before; L2/L3 and reconcile-running-set byte-unchanged in the diff. Tamper test red-to-green verified from n3's honest RED capture (#725a failed pre-change with the exact always-shells output quoted) and my own green rerun: `node scripts/test-adaptive-node.js` 2487 assertions exit 0. #725b pins true-recompute-not-marker-trust (setup asserts recompute != stale marker; refusal with zero mutation; fallback subprocess observed). The canonical walkthrough (with the #387 end-to-end tamper assertion) passed over the final tree in my own run. All four editions carry the fast path (gitlab/gitea diffs carry the forge-renamed validator require; script-sync + edition-sync --check green prove codex byte identity and forge-port parity).
3. (hooks) FAIL — this is the blocking frontier. The deletion itself is complete and correct: 8 files deleted, zero leftovers on disk, all six hooks.json now carry exactly SessionStart+SubagentStart (verified by parsing all six), both install-manifest SUPPORT_HOOKS narrowed (twins byte-identical per script-sync), install.sh carries the two rm -f upgrade sweeps mirroring the existing #372 precedent plus the fixed echo string (bash -n clean), the 5 contract validators dropped every named assert and all run green standalone over the final tree, validate-script-sync dropped exactly the two hook byte-copy groups, the four edition walkthroughs' hook sections removed, test-install-model-rendering's id set narrowed (suite green). The surviving compact-context + subagent-dispatch-log hooks are byte-untouched vs base (empty diff) and their assertions intact. BUT five assertion/consumer surfaces that the deletion trips were missed — R1-R5 above — so the four-chain element fails.
4. (docs) PASS. The 7-file delta removes the two hooks' live-behavior mentions: README hook tables 4→2 rows with 6 adjacent count-prose corrections, api.md Codex table + env bullets, architecture.md INV-2 clause, conventions.md gate-window section, workflow-state-contract.md kind:'gate' clauses, CLAUDE.md line 117; CLAUDE.md is 143 lines; CHANGELOG documents the hook deletion + edition-sync dedup + L1 fast path under [Unreleased] ### Removed with a single non-closing "(#725)". Hook-ID grep (pre-commit-guard|kaola-workflow:write-lane) over the 6 docs = 0 hits. No provenance leakage into agent-facing surfaces. validate-workflow-contracts green over the final docs (no Phase-A-style validator pin tripped).
5. (scope) PASS. opencode/kimi trees untouched (no re-sync); the diff contains zero opencode/kimi paths. R4 does not contradict this element — it shows the deferral's premise ("opencode is outside npm test") is false for the sync script specifically, which is exactly the replan input.
6. (green) FAIL. claude: red at step 2 (R3) and step 11 (R4); I ran every OTHER claude chain step individually over the final tree — all green, including test-adaptive-node (2487), test-edition-sync (40), test-route-reachability (2277), test-agent-profile-parity (461), test-run-chains (233), validate-workflow-contracts, the full canonical walkthrough ("Workflow walkthrough simulation passed"), test-active-folders-field-parity (61), generate-routing-surfaces --check, and test-generate-routing-surfaces (280) — so R3+R4 are the complete claude frontier. codex: validate-script-sync, validate-kaola-workflow-contracts, test-active-folders, gen-routing --check all green; the codex walkthrough reds only at R5. gitlab/gitea: reds proven precisely by the standalone forge-test runs (R1/R2) per the dispatch's cheap-reproduction instruction; full chains deliberately NOT run (their other steps — validate-vendored-agents, edition-sync --check, both forge contract validators — all ran green standalone here); the epoch-2 rerun owns the final all-four verification. Chain-run budget respected: one claude chain invocation (died at step 2; remaining steps covered individually), one codex tail run, zero full gitlab/gitea chain runs.

Upstream adjudications: n1's T10 removal — accepted; T10 called the removed checkMirrors directly and existed solely to test it, so leaving it would throw at runtime; the removal is inside n1's declared write set, n2-approved, and forge-aggregator parity coverage (T1-T8) is intact. n4's two non-blocking observations — concur with both non-admissions: the marker-case divergence admits no tampered content (the recompute still proves the covered bytes intact) and has no plausible accidental trigger; the residual-check bypass on a true match is the design boundary the frozen claim itself specifies. n6's judgment call — accepted on the merits: the 4 remaining past-tense mentions describe the two env flags (KAOLA_LANE_CONTAINMENT / KAOLA_GATE_WINDOW_FENCE) that genuinely still exist in adaptive-schema.js as plan-declared out-of-scope dead code; I reproduced the grep (exactly 4 hits: README.md, docs/conventions.md, docs/api.md x2) and each states the enforcing hook is removed and the flag has no runtime consumer — deleting the rows would misdocument the flags as nonexistent, and present tense would restate deleted behavior as live; this is the accurate current state, not a missed mention.

Non-blocking notes (not gate_claim obligations, recorded for the planner): (a) scripts/sync-kimi-edition.js:58-59,:422,:428 and the opencode/kimi test suites still reference the deleted hook basenames — additive editions, not in any of the four chains, explicitly deferred to Phase D by the frozen plan; unlike R4, nothing in the four chains shells the kimi sync (verified by grep), so kimi stays correctly deferred. (b) The adaptive-schema.js x4 and plan-validator x4 comment references to the write-lane machinery remain per the plan's explicit traps (out of scope, comment-only). (c) Suites that short-circuit at R1/R2/R5 could shadow further reds behind them; I bounded the known hook surface by repo-wide PreToolUse + basename sweeps (no further functional hits in the four chains), and the epoch-2 wall rerun provides the terminal proof. (d) The dispatch predicted this outcome as "domain_outcome: rejected"; the schema's closed approval vocabulary is approved/changes_requested, so the machine token above is changes_requested — same meaning, NOT approved.

Gate verdict: changes_requested. Four of the five blockers (R1-R4) anchor in files no plan node owns — the claim-preserving replan path the dispatch anticipated — and one (R5) anchors inside n5's declared write set and is an n5 reopen-in-place candidate. All five share one already-proven fix shape (narrow the assertion/list to the surviving two-hook surface), so a single repair epoch should close the frontier.

review_attestation: full_review_completed
review_conclusion: Phase C's substance is faithful — the dedup removes both double-checks with the unique coverage retained, the L1 in-process recompute provably preserves tamper refusal via the shared computePlanHash, the hook deletion and its declared wiring surface are complete across all four editions, and the docs delta is accurate including the justified past-tense flag rewrites — but the four-chain-green claim element is factually unmet: five missed assertion or consumer surfaces (the two forge PreToolUse hard-asserts, the script-sync fixture plant, the in-chain opencode sync's stale HOOK_SCRIPTS, and the codex walkthrough's stale four-hook count) red every one of the four chains, so the candidate is not finalize-gate-safe until the replan assigns the four unowned surfaces and n5 repairs its own missed assertion.
